machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin (secretion channel; forms a multimer)
machinery_genes.quote: The last ORF of an xps gene cluster, designated xpsD, is required for the secretion of extracellular enzymes across the outer membrane in Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris.
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 1995; XpsD = the secretin (GspD/PulD homolog, ORF3 of the founding 1992 paper PMID 1313415, which reported 51% identity to Klebsiella PulD). Outer-membrane lipoprotein that forms a multimeric secretion channel (Chen et al 1996, PMID 8576244).

machinery_genes.quote: ATP binding triggers oligomerization of Xanthomonas campestris XpsE (a GspE homolog) as well as its association with the N-terminal domain of XpsL (a GspL homolog)
machinery_genes.note: Shiue et al 2006; XpsE is the GspE-family secretion ATPase of the Xcc T2SS. Its essential N-terminal domain and ATP-coupling role are characterized further in Chen et al 2005 (PMID 16162504) and Shiue et al 2007 (PMID 17630971).

machinery_genes.quote: Upstream of ORF2, we have identified nine ORFs (unpublished results; accession no. L02630 and M81648), the deduced amino acid sequences of which have sequence homologies to various extents, as well as the same gene order, with the pulEFGHIJKLM genes of K. oxytoca.
machinery_genes.note: XpsF is the GspF-family IM platform component. No individual-characterization paper naming 'XpsF' was found; it is established as a cluster member by the homology/synteny of the nine xps ORFs upstream of xpsN (ORF2) with K. oxytoca pulEFGHIJKLM (pulF -> xpsF). Lower-confidence call, supported only by the cluster homology quote (Lee et al 2000).

machinery_genes.quote: we performed biochemical analyses of the XpsG protein of Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris and observed that it is a pillar-like structure spanning the cytoplasmic and outer membranes
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 2002; XpsG is the major pseudopilin (GspG-family) of the Xcc T2SS, forming a pilus-like structure. Title: 'XpsG, the major pseudopilin in Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris'. Confirmed as an essential apparatus component associating with XpsN and XpsD by cross-linking (Lee et al 2004, PMID 15590656).

machinery_genes.quote: the pseudopilins, XpsG, -H, -I, -J and -K, have been predicted to form a pilus-like structure in the type II secretion (T2S) pathway
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; names XpsH as one of the five Xcc pseudopilins (GspH-family). The paper characterizes the minor pseudopilins XpsH, XpsI, XpsJ and their interaction order XpsG-XpsI-XpsH-XpsJ.

machinery_genes.quote: the pseudopilins, XpsG, -H, -I, -J and -K, have been predicted to form a pilus-like structure in the type II secretion (T2S) pathway
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; names XpsI as a minor pseudopilin (GspI-family) of the Xcc T2SS.

machinery_genes.quote: the pseudopilins, XpsG, -H, -I, -J and -K, have been predicted to form a pilus-like structure in the type II secretion (T2S) pathway
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; names XpsJ as a minor pseudopilin (GspJ-family) of the Xcc T2SS; proposed to act as the pseudopilus tip restricting XpsG out-growth.

machinery_genes.quote: the pseudopilins, XpsG, -H, -I, -J and -K, have been predicted to form a pilus-like structure in the type II secretion (T2S) pathway
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; names XpsK as one of the five Xcc pseudopilins (GspK-family). Also covered as pulK homolog by the cluster-synteny quote in Lee et al 2000.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds the ATPase XpsE)
machinery_genes.quote: The cytoplasmic membrane proteins XpsL, XpsM and XpsN are components required for type II secretion in Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris.
machinery_genes.note: Tsai et al 2002; XpsL (GspL-family) is one of three IM-platform proteins forming a ternary XpsL-XpsM-XpsN complex. XpsL anchors the ATPase XpsE to the membrane via its N-terminal domain (Shiue et al 2006, PMID 16525507).

machinery_genes.quote: The cytoplasmic membrane proteins XpsL, XpsM and XpsN are components required for type II secretion in Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris.
machinery_genes.note: Tsai et al 2002; XpsM (GspM-family) IM-platform protein; mutual steady-state dependence with XpsL demonstrated in Lee et al 2001 (PMID 11133946).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component linking IM platform to the secretin (GspC)
machinery_genes.quote: XpsL, XpsM, and XpsN are components of such machinery in the plant pathogen Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris.
machinery_genes.note: Lee et al 2004 ('Functional dissection of the XpsN (GspC) protein of the Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris type II secretion machinery'); XpsN is the GspC-family component. It bridges the XpsL-XpsM IM complex (Lee et al 2000, PMID 10692359) and the outer-membrane secretin XpsD (Lee et al 2000).

machinery_genes.role: type IV prepilin/pseudopilin leader peptidase (matures pseudopilins; shared with type IV pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: Sequencing of the cloned fragment revealed an open reading frame (ORF) (designated xpsO) of 287 amino acid residues... Type IV pre-pilin leader peptidase was demonstrated to be required for protein secretion
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 1995; XpsO = the X. campestris type IV prepilin leader peptidase (PilD/XcpA/GspO homolog, 50% identity to PilD), required for extracellular protein secretion via the Xps T2SS. Encoded outside the xpsE-N cluster but functionally part of the apparatus (matures the pseudopilins).

notes: Xps system = the archetypal Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris (Xcc) T2SS, characterized gene-by-gene over ~15 years by the Hu/Leu/Chen group at National Chung Hsing University. All apparatus papers used Xcc pv. campestris strains in the Xc17/XC1701/XC1708/8004 lineage (same pathovar and same Xps system as the sequenced reference strain ATCC 33913 = str. 8004-related; the xps locus is conserved across pv. campestris), so component naming transfers directly to this instance. The founding paper (Hu et al 1992, PMID 1313415) cloned the cluster and identified xpsD (ORF3) as a PulD homolog; Lee et al 2000 (PMID 10692359) established that the nine ORFs upstream of xpsN (ORF2) are syntenic homologs of K. oxytoca pulEFGHIJKLM, fixing the xpsE-M assignment, with xpsN and xpsD completing the 11-ORF cluster. Core apparatus fully covered: secretin (XpsD), ATPase (XpsE), IM platform (XpsF, XpsL, XpsM, XpsN/GspC), major + minor pseudopilins (XpsG, XpsH, XpsI, XpsJ, XpsK), and the prepilin peptidase (XpsO, encoded outside the cluster, shared with the type IV pilus). XpsF is the only gene without an individual-characterization quote; it is supported by the cluster-synteny homology to pulF and flagged as the lowest-confidence call (mirrors the EpsN treatment in the T2SS_01 pilot). Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the secreted substrates/effectors (alpha-amylase, polygalacturonate lyase / pglA, endoglucanase, protease, XCC2387), the upstream Sec/Tat translocon, and the transcriptional regulator Clp/XpsE-promoter regulation (PMID 18175178). All quotes are verbatim from PubMed abstracts/titles or PMC text of the cited papers.
